codeReview.summary: Solid implementation of semantic memory with graph-based knowledge representation. Entities and relations are properly stored with typed nodes/edges. 12 unit tests cover core functionality. Code is clean, compiles without warnings, and follows project conventions.

issues.description: SemanticRelationResult struct is defined but never used - dead code. Either implement a method that returns it or remove it.
SemanticEntityType::parse() silently returns SemanticEntityType::Project as default for unknown strings instead of returning an error. This could mask typos in entity type strings.
SemanticRelationType::parse() silently returns SemanticRelationType::References as default for unknown strings instead of returning an error. Same issue as above.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker followed project conventions correctly - async_trait for async trait methods, proper error handling with SwellError, Arc<dyn Trait> for dynamic dispatch.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: semantic.rs uses async_trait::async_trait, returns Result<T, SwellError>, uses SqlitePool with Arc

sharedStateObservations.observation: Skill feedback indicates procedure was followed correctly. Worker used appropriate Rust patterns for async database operations.
No knowledge gaps identified - implementation uses standard sqlx patterns consistent with other modules in swell-memory
sharedStateObservations.evidence: semantic.rs follows same patterns as blocks.rs, recall.rs for SQLite operations

summary: mem-semantic-memory feature passes review. Implementation provides graph-based semantic memory with SemanticEntity (18 types) and SemanticRelation (14 types) via SqliteSemanticStore. All expected behaviors implemented: entity/relation CRUD, query by type, query by name, cascade delete. 12 tests pass. Three non-blocking issues: unused SemanticRelationResult struct and lenient parse() defaults that could mask typos.
